# shuriken-sdk

> The official SDK for apps on the Metanet social network at metanet.page. A hybrid typed / `call()` bridge to the parent platform over a signed, JSON-RPC-style postMessage protocol. Works in React or any JS/TS project. Zero runtime deps. 12 methods, closed surface.

Init once with `connect()`, then use typed sugar (`ninja.pay.bsv`) or the uniform core (`ninja.call('pay', params)`). Identity is version-discriminated (V0 wallet / V1 purpose-keys); anchor on `canonicalId`. Every response is signature-verified before it resolves.

## Start here
- [README](./README.md): install, the hybrid model, quickstart, per-capability recipes, errors, React.
- [AGENTS](./AGENTS.md): task-oriented playbook; the V0/V1 identity trap up top.
- [PROTOCOL](./PROTOCOL.md): exact wire envelope, correlation, timeouts, signatures, streaming, handshake.
- [manifest.json](./manifest.json): machine-readable source of truth — every method schema, error code, consent + streaming flags.

## Methods (wire type → sugar)
- connection → `ninja.connect({ request?, proofs?, salt?, navbg? })` → version-discriminated identity. Purposes: `request` takes `bsv|icp|kda|content`; `proofs` adds `app`. Core set today — future platform versions may add more (incl. custom namespaces); unknown purposes are forwarded untouched. RE-CALLABLE = the canonical ask-later pattern: approved items resolve silently; anything not yet approved re-prompts the full list (ONLY approvals are stored — declines are never remembered)
- pay → `ninja.pay.bsv(recipients, {broadcast?})` | `ninja.pay.icp({token,to,amount})` | `ninja.pay.kda({to,amount})` — bsv broadcast defaults true (finalizes on-chain → txid); `{broadcast:false}` returns authorized-only rawTxHex, finalize via `broadcastRawTx(rawTxHex, me.genericUseSeed)`
- create-post → `ninja.feed.createPost({ headline, nftDescription, previewAsset?, appEmbed? })` → `{ postId }` — nftDescription = the post BODY (required); headline = short title
- generate-proof → `ninja.proof.generate({ reason? })` → Groth16 APP-identity proof (app-only shortcut, no purpose param; other purposes via `ninja.connect({ proofs })`)
- full-transaction → `ninja.tx.get(txid)` → `{ txid, rawHex, bumpHex }`
- token-history → `ninja.tx.history({ chain?, limit?, offset? })`
- geolocation (stream) → `ninja.geo.current()` | `ninja.geo.watch()` (fix = {latitude,longitude,accuracy?,isFinal?}); stop via break
- qr-scan → `ninja.qr.scan(onResult)` — fires once then auto-closes the camera; `.stop()` cancels early
- open-link → `ninja.openLink(url)`
- write-clipboard → `ninja.clipboard.write(text)` (no response)
- uniform core for any method → `ninja.call(method, params, { timeoutMs?, signal? })`

## Rules
- Identity: branch on `me.anonymous`, then `me.version` (0 | 1). Only `canonicalId` exists on both. No `wallet || identities` fallback.
- V1 purposes: `app` (session signer, pub only) + `bsv`/`icp`/`kda` (chain id-field: address/principal/account) + `content` (pure key purpose — pub only, no chain field). Unknown/future purposes pass through verbatim on `me` and via `me.raw`.
- Errors: catch `NinjaError`, branch on `err.code` (closed union), localize with `t(err.code)`. `ERR_ABORTED` = user cancel.
- ICP/KDA payments are single-recipient. App ZK proofs require V1.
- ZK proofs are verified CLIENT-SIDE out of the box (Groth16 pairing vs SHA-pinned embedded vkeys): `connect()`/`proof.generate()` auto-verify and reject tampered proofs with `ERR_PROOF_INVALID` (`ERR_VKEY_INTEGRITY` = corrupted bundle, fail closed); manual: `verifyIdentityProof(envelope, canonicalId, pub)`; opt-out flags `verifyProofs`/`verifyProof` (client-side only).
- `connect()` (init/transport) then `ninja.connect()` (identity) — different calls.

## Optional
- [MIGRATION](./docs/MIGRATION.md): converting a legacy hand-rolled ninja SDK app to the typed shuriken-sdk API (wire unchanged; client-side refactor).
- `decodeCanonicalId(canonicalId)` → `{ version, anchorHex, seedCommitment? }` (also `ninja.identity.decodeCanonicalId`): decodes BOTH identity versions of `me.canonicalId` = base58(versionByte||anchor), hyphen-grouped — V0 `0x00||hash160(pubkey)` (anchorHex = 40-hex pkh), V1 `0x01||seedCommitment` (32-byte field). Pure decode, no ZK check. Always key user data on `me.canonicalId`.
